name: Casbah of Algiers
description: The Ottoman-era citadel and old medina of Algiers, a UNESCO World Heritage Site of steep alleys, traditional houses, mosques and palaces overlooking the Mediterranean bay.

name: Tassili n'Ajjer
description: A vast sandstone plateau deep in the Sahara, UNESCO-listed for thousands of prehistoric rock paintings and engravings and its dramatic eroded rock 'forests'.

name: Timgad
description: The 'Pompeii of Africa' - a remarkably preserved Roman colonial town founded by Emperor Trajan around 100 AD, famous for its grid plan, theatre and Arch of Trajan.

name: Djemila (Cuicul)
description: Mountain Roman ruins adapted to rugged terrain, with a well-preserved forum, temples, basilicas and exceptional mosaics. A UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Tipaza
description: Seaside ruins blending Phoenician, Roman and early-Christian remains on the Mediterranean coast, near the Royal Mausoleum of Mauretania. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: M'zab Valley (Ghardaia)
description: Five fortified hilltop towns (ksour) built by the Mozabites around the 10th century, with distinctive cubic architecture and palm groves. A UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Hoggar Mountains (Assekrem)
description: A volcanic highland range in the deep south, famous for the Assekrem viewpoint and its spectacular Saharan sunrises, and for Tuareg culture.

name: Notre-Dame d'Afrique
description: A 19th-century Neo-Byzantine Roman Catholic basilica perched above the bay of Algiers, known for its inscription 'Pray for us and for the Muslims'.

name: Maqam Echahid (Martyrs' Memorial)
description: A towering 1982 concrete monument shaped like three palm fronds, commemorating the Algerian War of Independence, with an eternal flame and panoramic views.

name: Santa Cruz Fort
description: A 16th-century Spanish fortress on Mount Murdjadjo above Oran, beside the Santa Cruz chapel, offering sweeping views over the city and harbour.

name: Jardin d'Essai du Hamma
description: A historic botanical garden founded in 1832, with grand avenues of palms and ficus, rare plant collections and a small zoo near the bay.

name: Constantine - Bridges & Rhumel Gorge
description: The dramatic 'City of Bridges', built on a plateau split by the deep Rhumel gorge and linked by spectacular suspension bridges such as Sidi M'Cid.

name: Tlemcen - Great Mosque & El Mechouar
description: A jewel of Andalusian-Moorish heritage: the 12th-century Almoravid Great Mosque and the restored El Mechouar palace-citadel.

name: Cap Carbon (Bejaia)
description: A rugged Mediterranean cape in Gouraya National Park, with sea cliffs and one of the highest natural lighthouses in the world.

name: Basilica of St. Augustine (Annaba)
description: A 19th-century hilltop basilica overlooking the ruins of ancient Hippo Regius, where St. Augustine served as bishop.
